seoTitle: Carlton vs Carlton North — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Carlton and Carlton North. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Carlton vs Carlton North: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Carlton and Carlton North.
heading: Carlton vs Carlton North — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Carlton, Victoria and Carlton North, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Carlton has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Carlton North (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Carlton or Carlton North safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Carlton has a flood risk score of 60/100, while Carlton North scores 51/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Carlton scores 56/100 for bushfire risk. Carlton North scores 55/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Carlton or Carlton North?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Carlton has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.58% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
